user_query:
I need to turn our latest weekly coordination meeting into clear outputs I can file and share. Please work from the provided inputs and produce the following artifacts under the specified paths.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/weekly_meeting_2026-04-15.md — raw meeting notes with attendance, decisions, risks, and action items.
- input/progress_plan_actual.csv — planned vs actual percentages by work package for the meeting date.
- input/contacts.json — subcontractor email recipients.
- input/project_info.yaml — project name, site address, and meeting date/time.

Required outputs:
1) Compute progress variance via a script/CLI you create and run:
   - Read input/progress_plan_actual.csv and write output/progress_variance.csv with these columns: date, work_package, planned_pct, actual_pct, delta_pct (actual_pct - planned_pct), status (Behind if actual_pct < planned_pct; On Track if equal; Ahead if actual_pct > planned_pct).
   - Record the exact command you ran to generate output/progress_variance.csv in output/run_commands.txt (one line is fine).

2) Meeting summary for filing:
   - Create output/weekly_site_meeting_summary.md that includes the following sections populated from input/weekly_meeting_2026-04-15.md and output/progress_variance.csv:
     - Header with project_name and meeting_date from input/project_info.yaml.
     - Attendance list (as captured in the notes).
     - Key Decisions.
     - Risks/Issues.
     - Progress Summary: for each work_package in output/progress_variance.csv, list planned_pct, actual_pct, delta_pct, and status. The figures in this section must match the values in output/progress_variance.csv.
     - Action Items table: extract every line that begins with "Action:" from the notes and tabulate ID, Owner, Due date (YYYY-MM-DD), and Description. Include all action items found in the notes.

3) Action item tracker for follow-up:
   - Create output/action_items.csv with columns: id, owner, due_date, description. Populate it from the same "Action:" lines in input/weekly_meeting_2026-04-15.md so the entries match the Action Items table in the summary.

4) Draft subcontractor emails for their action items:
   - For each subcontractor listed in input/contacts.json that has at least one action item in the notes, draft one plain-text email saved to output/emails/email_<Company_Name_With_Spaces_Replaced_By_Underscores>.txt (e.g., "output/emails/email_Apex_Concrete.txt").
   - Each email must include:
     - A "To:" line listing the recipient email(s) from input/contacts.json for that company.
     - A "Subject:" line in the form: "<project_name> — Action items from <meeting_date>" using values from input/project_info.yaml.
     - A brief body that: (a) states the meeting date and project name; (b) lists each action for that company with ID and due date, and a one-line description; and (c) asks for confirmation of receipt and planned completion.
   - Only include action items owned by that subcontractor; do not include internal team items.

Notes and constraints:
- Running a script or command-line program to generate output/progress_variance.csv is required; the meeting summary must reference the computed values from that CSV.
- Do not invent or modify facts beyond what’s in the inputs; if a field is missing from an input, omit it rather than guessing.
- Keep file paths exactly as specified above.

Deliverables checklist (for clarity only):
- output/progress_variance.csv
- output/run_commands.txt
- output/weekly_site_meeting_summary.md
- output/action_items.csv
- output/emails/email_Apex_Concrete.txt (expected, if Apex Concrete has actions)
- output/emails/email_NorthStar_Electrical.txt (expected, if NorthStar Electrical has actions) All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
